headline: Citi flags upside catalysts for Delta and SkyWest over the next 30 days
summary: Investing.com -- Citi told investors in separate notes Thursday that it has placed Delta Air Lines and SkyWest on 30-day upside catalyst watch, arguing that the recent macro shock and fuel volatility are now increasingly priced in for carriers with the least exposure to jet-fuel costs.

content.title: Citi flags upside catalysts for Delta and SkyWest over the next 30 days

headline: Delta Air Lines Price Prediction: One Wall Street Analyst Sees 50% Upside This Year
summary: Delta Air Lines (NYSE:DAL) has had a rough stretch heading into mid-March. Shares are down 2.31% over the past week, off 16.62% over the past month and down 16.09% year-to-date. Trading around $57.95, the stock’s 52-week high was $76.39. But with Wall Street’s consensus target sitting at $81.69, a meaningful recovery could be in store ... Delta Air Lines Price Prediction: One Wall Street Analyst Sees 50% Upside This Year

headline: Airline stocks slide as oil surges past $100 on Iran tanker attacks
summary: Southwest Airlines (NYSE:LUV) led declines, dropping 2.7% to $40.73 after ending its fuel hedging program in 2025, leaving it fully exposed to spot market pricing. Delta Air Lines Inc (NYSE:DAL) fell 2% to $57.97, while United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL) declined 2.2% to $88.73. American Airlines Group (BMV:AAL) fell 2% to $10.82. European airline stocks suffered steeper losses, falling between 2.4% and 5.8% Thursday morning, with Lufthansa, Air France-KLM, easyJet, IAG, Wizz Air, Norwegian Air, and R
